title: Did decaying dark matter help create the universe's first supermassive black holes?
summary: Decaying dark matter may have contributed to the formation of the universe's first supermassive black holes, as suggested by observations from the James Webb Space Telescope.

title: The 1st 'Alien: Isolation 2' teaser is here, and the setting looks eerily familiar
summary: The first teaser for 'Alien: Isolation 2' has been released, featuring a familiar setting that evokes a dark and intense atmosphere.

title: All 4 original Alien movie special editions are now on HBO Max; here's how they differ from the theatrical cuts
summary: All four original Alien movie special editions have been added to HBO Max, highlighting the differences between theatrical and director's cuts. This move celebrates Alien Day, offering fans a chance to compare the alternate versions.

title: Watch comet PANSTARRS 'switch on' its second tail as it makes closest approach to Earth
summary: Comet C/2025 R3 PANSTARRS made its closest approach to Earth at 45 million miles, revealing a newly activated ion tail. The event was observed as the comet transitioned through space.

title: SpaceX launches its 50th mission of the year, sends 25 Starlink satellites to orbit (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its 50th mission of the year, deploying 25 Starlink satellites into orbit from California. This marks another milestone in the company's rapid launch cadence.

title: We went hands-on with 'The Expanse: Osiris Reborn', and it's a hard sci-fi 'Mass Effect' successor with show-stealing zero-G sections
summary: Owlcat Games' 'The Expanse: Osiris Reborn' is a hard sci-fi successor to 'Mass Effect' with impressive zero-G gameplay sections. The game builds on the existing 'The Expanse' title, offering a promising experience for fans of the genre.

title: Watch Atlas V rocket launch 29 Amazon internet satellites to orbit today
summary: A United Launch Alliance Atlas V rocket is scheduled to launch 29 of Amazon's internet satellites into orbit today. The event can be watched live.

title: Backyard snapshot delivers stunning galaxy image | Space photo of the day for April 27, 2026
summary: A stunning space photo captures the Small Magellanic Cloud, a neighboring galaxy of the Milky Way. The image was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for April 27, 2026.

title: Astrobotic fires next-generation 'rotating detonation rocket engine' in record-breaking test (video)
summary: Astrobotic successfully tested its next-generation rotating detonation rocket engine, achieving a record-breaking milestone for the experimental propulsion technology. The hot-fire tests represent a significant advancement in advanced rocket engine development.

title: NASA’s X-59 Gets Freedom 250 Logo
summary: NASA's X-59 research aircraft has received a special livery to commemorate the U.S. 250th independence anniversary. The aircraft is part of the Quesst mission to demonstrate quiet supersonic flight technology.

title: NASA’s Perseverance, Curiosity Panoramas Capture Two Sides of Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity and Perseverance rovers have captured 360-degree panoramas of Mars, revealing details about the planet's formation, watery past, and potential for life. The rovers are exploring regions 2,345 miles apart, comparable to the distance between Los Angeles and Washington, D.C.

title: You Can Help Humans Thrive in Space
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission marked the first crewed deep-space flight since 1972, carrying four astronauts around the moon. The agency invites the public to contribute to human space exploration through citizen science projects.

title: Volunteers Help NASA Astronauts Record Lunar Flashes
summary: NASA's Artemis II astronauts observed lunar flashes caused by meteoroid impacts, while volunteers for the Impact Flash project recorded these events from Earth using telescopes.

title: I Am Artemis: Peter Rossoni
summary: Peter Rossoni, Orion Artemis II Optical Communications System flight manager, reflects on his journey from watching Apollo missions as a child to contributing to NASA's Artemis II mission in April 2026. His role involves enabling communications for astronauts during their lunar journey.

title: The Day of the Trifid Nebula
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a detailed image of the Trifid Nebula, 5,000 light-years from Earth, to celebrate the mission's 36th launch anniversary on April 24, 2026.

title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Missouri Students
summary: NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Jack Hathaway will answer STEM questions from Missouri students during a live Earth-to-space call from the International Space Station on April 30. The event will be streamed on NASA's Learn With NASA YouTube channel.

title: NASA Celebrates Decade of University Innovation in Aeronautics
summary: NASA's University Leadership Initiative (ULI) celebrates 10 years of fostering aeronautical innovation and workforce development, supporting over 1,100 students across 100 schools.

title: Johnson Leaders Honored by National Space Club & Foundation
summary: Two leaders from NASA's Johnson Space Center were honored by the National Space Club & Foundation for their contributions to human spaceflight. Howard Hu received the Norman L. Baker Astronautics Engineer Award for his technical achievements.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: Falcon Heavy, Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites this week from multiple locations. SpaceX's Falcon Heavy is making a return for these launches.

title: SpaceX scrubs launch of Falcon Heavy on Monday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX postponed the launch of its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. The scrub marks a delay for the heavy-lift vehicle's return to flight.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur Cosmodrome to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the recent CRS NG-24 mission.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the Starship rocket at the Cape, aiming for a launch this year as the first flight of Block 3 approaches.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The OIG report raises concerns about the program's timeline and potential impacts on future missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches, are scheduled for the week of April 20 across four countries using five different launch vehicles. The missions highlight a busy period in spaceflight activity.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after the launch of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for the company's satellite communications ambitions.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch. This marks a significant milestone in the development of partially reusable orbital launch systems.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an astronaut flight suit and a large docking adapter, combining fashion with practicality for future space missions. The innovations support the development of commercial space stations.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication showcases the agency's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully passed space-like tests, remaining on schedule for a launch in early 2027 to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on 8-9 June 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received at an optimal location. This milestone marks a significant advancement in navigation technology.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands, scheduled for 16–17 September.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved accuracy or efficiency in directional control. It highlights innovations that could enhance mission performance or reduce operational costs.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated laser-propelled graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's 2025 parabolic flight campaign, showing potential for future satellite propulsion. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, found that laser strength directly controlled acceleration in space-like conditions.

title: Meta Secures Overview Energy Space Solar Power Capacity
summary: Meta has secured an agreement to obtain up to 1 GW of power capacity through Overview Energy's planned solar power-beaming satellite system. This deal marks Meta's entry into space-based solar power initiatives.

title: Pentagon Taps 12 Companies for Golden Dome SBI Tech
summary: The Pentagon has selected 12 companies for the Golden Dome SBI Tech program, awarding contracts worth up to $3.2 billion in total. This initiative aims to advance space-based technology and defense capabilities.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to drive cost reductions for commercial and government customers. The initiative targets the 'Golden Dome,' a metaphor for scaling operations.

title: Trump Taps Space Execs For Military Space Roles
summary: Former President Trump has appointed two space industry executives to key military space roles. The nominees require Senate confirmation before assuming their positions.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center. The engine could potentially power future Moon missions.

title: Orbital vs. Terrestrial Data Center Cost Analysis
summary: The article compares the build costs of orbital and terrestrial data centers, including the opportunity cost of delays for terrestrial options. It is published by Payload Pro.

title: UNIVITY Raises €27M Series A
summary: UNIVITY has raised €27 million in a Series A funding round led by Blast Club and Expansion Venture Capital, with additional support from Bpifrance's Deep Tech fund.

title: Lawmakers Promise to Reject Proposed NASA Cuts—Again
summary: Lawmakers have vowed to reject proposed budget cuts to NASA, arguing that the cuts would hinder the agency's ability to meet President Trump's directives. The statement comes as a response to a new budget proposal that has raised concerns among legislators.

title: Tracking Sats From the Sea
summary: The article discusses how satellites provide domain awareness to protect Navy ships and suggests that the Navy could assist in tracking these satellites. It highlights the mutual benefits of collaboration between the Navy and satellite tracking efforts.

title: ATMOS Space Cargo Raises €25.7M Series A
summary: ATMOS Space Cargo secured €25.7 million in Series A funding to transition to regular operational service. The company is developing commercial, institutional, and defense-capable space cargo architectures with ATMOS WORKS and PHOENIX 3.

title: ULA Launches 29 Satellites in 6th Mission for Amazon Leo
summary: United Launch Alliance (ULA) launched 29 satellites for Amazon's Project Kuiper on an Atlas V rocket. This was ULA's sixth mission supporting Amazon's satellite constellation.

title: Gatehouse Satcom and Rohde & Schwarz Partner on 5GN NTN Testing
summary: Gatehouse Satcom and Rohde & Schwarz have partnered to collaborate on 5GN non-terrestrial network (NTN) testing, leveraging Gatehouse's software expertise and Rohde & Schwarz's testing capabilities.

title: HawkEye 360 Files to Go Public, Reports $99M in Revenue in 2025
summary: HawkEye 360, a signals intelligence company, announced plans to go public in 2024 with a road show for an IPO. The company reported projected revenue of $99 million for 2025.

title: Pakistan Adds to EO Fleet of Satellites
summary: Pakistan launched the EO-3 satellite, expanding its Earth Observation (EO) fleet. The Pakistan Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission (SUPARCO) conducted the launch to enhance national EO capabilities.

title: Space Force Awards $3.2 Billion to 12 Companies for Space-Based Interceptor Work
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded contracts totaling up to $3.2 billion to 12 companies for developing space-based interceptor technologies under the Golden Dome initiative. This move aims to enhance space defense capabilities.

title: GomSpace to Establish New Ukrainian Joint Venture for Sovereign Comms
summary: GomSpace, a Danish satellite company, is establishing a new joint venture in Ukraine to develop sovereign satellite communications capabilities. The partnership will involve GomSpace and its Ukrainian partner Stetman.

title: Rocket Lab Introduces Next-Generation Star Tracker Component
summary: Rocket Lab has launched its next-generation High-Performance Star Tracker (ST-HP) component, designed for extended spacecraft missions. This expansion strengthens the company's offerings in space navigation technology.

title: FCC Rejects Requests for Sharing in MSS Spectrum, Maintaining Exclusive Rights
summary: The FCC rejected requests from SpaceX and other operators to share MSS spectrum bands, maintaining exclusive licensing rights. The decision was formalized in an order issued on Thursday.

title: SDA’s Need for Speed Pushes Startups for Results
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) is pausing launches until May to address technology hurdles, supply chain challenges, and balance speed. Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo emphasized the need for startups to deliver results.

title: SpinLaunch to Use Equinx Data Centers as Teleport Sites for its Constellation
summary: SpinLaunch will collaborate with Equinx to deploy teleport sites at Equinx's data centers to support its Meridian constellation. The partnership aims to enhance ground infrastructure for the space industry startup.
